{{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Ōno Shrine''' (大野神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Rittō|Rittō]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |title=Ōno Shrine Festival |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Nihon Kankō Shinkō Kyōkai |archive-date=2018-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181119173520/http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |url-status=unfit |url-status-date=2023-03-30}}</ref> Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|鄉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Enshrined Deity ==
* Principal deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020223615/http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |archive-date=2020-10-20}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō]] 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It was later associated with [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

Throughout its history, the shrine was patronized by powerful clans such as the Sasaki (Rokkaku) family during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], and by [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshihisa|Ashikaga Yoshihisa]] in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2023-03-30 |publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208205908/https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |archive-date=2022-12-08}}</ref>

== Grounds ==
Main buildings include:
* Main Hall - Sangensha [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]] (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR [[:en:Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|Kusatsu Station]] by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of [[:en:Arashi|Arashi (group)]]'s leader, [[:en:Satoshi Ohno|Satoshi Ōno]], due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

It was later associated with [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

{{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Ōno Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Rittō|en|Rittō|WD=Q3937811|lt=Rittō}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |title=Ōno Shrine Festival |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Nihon Kankō Shinkō Kyōkai |archive-date=2018-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181119173520/http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |url-status=unfit |url-status-date=2023-03-30}}</ref> Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|鄉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Enshrined Deity ==
* Principal deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020223615/http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |archive-date=2020-10-20}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō}} 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

Throughout its history, the shrine was patronized by powerful clans such as the Sasaki (Rokkaku) family during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, and by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|en|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|WD=Q447639|lt=Ashikaga Yoshihisa}} in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2023-03-30 |publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208205908/https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |archive-date=2022-12-08}}</ref>

== Grounds ==
Main buildings include:
* Main Hall - Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}}
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon}} (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Ōno Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Rittō|en|Rittō|WD=Q3937811|lt=Rittō}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |title=Ōno Shrine Festival |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Nihon Kankō Shinkō Kyōkai |archive-date=2018-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181119173520/http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |url-status=unfit |url-status-date=2023-03-30}}</ref> Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|鄉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

* Principal deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020223615/http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |archive-date=2020-10-20}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō}} 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

|Established   = Late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}

{{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Ōno Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Rittō|en|Rittō|WD=Q3937811|lt=Rittō}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |title=Ōno Shrine Festival |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Nihon Kankō Shinkō Kyōkai |archive-date=2018-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181119173520/http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |url-status=unfit |url-status-date=2023-03-30}}</ref> Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|鄉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Enshrined Deity ==
* Principal deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020223615/http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |archive-date=2020-10-20}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō}} 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

Throughout its history, the shrine was patronized by powerful clans such as the Sasaki (Rokkaku) family during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, and by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|en|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|WD=Q447639|lt=Ashikaga Yoshihisa}} in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2023-03-30 |publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208205908/https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |archive-date=2022-12-08}}</ref>

== Grounds ==
Main buildings include:
* Main Hall - Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}}
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon}} (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

{{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Ōno Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Rittō|en|Rittō|WD=Q3937811|lt=Rittō}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |title=Ōno Shrine Festival |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Nihon Kankō Shinkō Kyōkai |archive-date=2018-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181119173520/http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |url-status=unfit |url-status-date=2023-03-30}}</ref> Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|鄉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Enshrined Deity ==
* Principal deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020223615/http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |archive-date=2020-10-20}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō}} 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

Throughout its history, the shrine was patronized by powerful clans such as the Sasaki (Rokkaku) family during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, and by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|en|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|WD=Q447639|lt=Ashikaga Yoshihisa}} in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2023-03-30 |publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208205908/https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |archive-date=2022-12-08}}</ref>

== Grounds ==
Main buildings include:
* Main Hall - Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}}
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon}} (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

* Principal deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020223615/http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |archive-date=2020-10-20}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō}} 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

Throughout its history, the shrine was patronized by powerful clans such as the Sasaki (Rokkaku) family during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, and by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|en|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|WD=Q447639|lt=Ashikaga Yoshihisa}} in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2023-03-30 |publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208205908/https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |archive-date=2022-12-08}}</ref>

== Grounds ==
Main buildings include:
* Main Hall - Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}}
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon}} (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

{{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Ōno Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Rittō|en|Rittō|WD=Q3937811|lt=Rittō}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |title=Ōno Shrine Festival |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Nihon Kankō Shinkō Kyōkai |archive-date=2018-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181119173520/http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |url-status=unfit |url-status-date=2023-03-30}}</ref> Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|鄉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Enshrined Deity ==
* Principal deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020223615/http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |archive-date=2020-10-20}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō}} 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

Throughout its history, the shrine was patronized by powerful clans such as the Sasaki (Rokkaku) family during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, and by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|en|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|WD=Q447639|lt=Ashikaga Yoshihisa}} in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2023-03-30 |publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208205908/https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |archive-date=2022-12-08}}</ref>

== Grounds ==
Main buildings include:
* Main Hall - Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}}
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon}} (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

{{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Ōno Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Rittō|en|Rittō|WD=Q3937811|lt=Rittō}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |title=Ōno Shrine Festival |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Nihon Kankō Shinkō Kyōkai |archive-date=2018-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181119173520/http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |url-status=unfit |url-status-date=2023-03-30}}</ref> Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|鄉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Enshrined Deity ==
* Principal deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020223615/http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |archive-date=2020-10-20}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō}} 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

Throughout its history, the shrine was patronized by powerful clans such as the Sasaki (Rokkaku) family during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, and by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|en|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|WD=Q447639|lt=Ashikaga Yoshihisa}} in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2023-03-30 |publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208205908/https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |archive-date=2022-12-08}}</ref>

== Grounds ==
Main buildings include:
* Main Hall - Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}}
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon}} (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

{{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Ōno Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Rittō|en|Rittō|WD=Q3937811|lt=Rittō|1=Rittō}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |title=Ōno Shrine Festival |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Nihon Kankō Shinkō Kyōkai |archive-date=2018-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181119173520/http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |url-status=unfit |url-status-date=2023-03-30}}</ref> Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|鄉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Enshrined Deity ==
* Principal deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020223615/http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |archive-date=2020-10-20}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō|1=Gangyō}} 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

Throughout its history, the shrine was patronized by powerful clans such as the Sasaki (Rokkaku) family during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, and by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|en|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|WD=Q447639|lt=Ashikaga Yoshihisa|1=Ashikaga Yoshihisa}} in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2023-03-30 |publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208205908/https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |archive-date=2022-12-08}}</ref>

== Grounds ==
Main buildings include:
* Main Hall - Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station|1=Kusatsu Station (Shiga)}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)|1=Arashi}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno|1=Satoshi Ohno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

== Notes and References ==
{{Reflist}}

== External Links ==
* [https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/ Official Site]

{{Shinto navbox}}

* {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|WD=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} 
* {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one)
* {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}}

It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

* Main Hall - Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station|1=Kusatsu Station (Shiga)}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)|1=Arashi}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno|1=Satoshi Ohno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]

| type                  = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]
|Name   = Ōno Shrine
|image   = File:OhnoJinja20190204.jpg
|Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|Rittō|en|Rittō|WD=Q3937811|lt=Rittō|1=Rittō}} Arabari 896
| Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 59 | Latitude Seconds = 23.9 | N/S = N
| Longitude Degrees = 136 | Longitude Minutes = 01 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | E/W = E
| Map Country Code = JP
| Map = Japan Shiga
| Label Position = left
|Deity   = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
|Shrine Rank   = Former {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|鄉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|Established   = Late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}
|Main Hall   = Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
|Other Names   = Tenjin Shrine, Komasaka Tenjin
|Main Festival   = May 5
|Shrine Events   =

}}
{{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Ōno Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Rittō|en|Rittō|WD=Q3937811|lt=Rittō|1=Rittō}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |title=Ōno Shrine Festival |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Nihon Kankō Shinkō Kyōkai |archive-date=2018-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181119173520/http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |url-status=unfit |url-status-date=2023-03-30}}</ref> Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|鄉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Enshrined Deity ==
* Principal deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020223615/http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |archive-date=2020-10-20}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō|1=Gangyō}} 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

Throughout its history, the shrine was patronized by powerful clans such as the Sasaki (Rokkaku) family during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, and by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|en|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|WD=Q447639|lt=Ashikaga Yoshihisa|1=Ashikaga Yoshihisa}} in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2023-03-30 |publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208205908/https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |archive-date=2022-12-08}}</ref>

== Grounds ==
Main buildings include:
* Main Hall - Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station|1=Kusatsu Station (Shiga)}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)|1=Arashi}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno|1=Satoshi Ohno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

{{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Ōno Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Rittō|en|Rittō|WD=Q3937811|lt=Rittō|1=Rittō}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |title=Ōno Shrine Festival |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Nihon Kankō Shinkō Kyōkai |archive-date=2018-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181119173520/http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |url-status=unfit |url-status-date=2023-03-30}}</ref> Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|鄉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Enshrined Deity ==
* Principal deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020223615/http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |archive-date=2020-10-20}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō|1=Gangyō}} 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

Throughout its history, the shrine was patronized by powerful clans such as the Sasaki (Rokkaku) family during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, and by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|en|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|WD=Q447639|lt=Ashikaga Yoshihisa|1=Ashikaga Yoshihisa}} in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2023-03-30 |publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208205908/https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |archive-date=2022-12-08}}</ref>

== Grounds ==
Main buildings include:
* Main Hall - Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station|1=Kusatsu Station (Shiga)}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)|1=Arashi}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno|1=Satoshi Ohno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō|1=Gangyō}} 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

Throughout its history, the shrine was patronized by powerful clans such as the Sasaki (Rokkaku) family during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, and by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|en|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|WD=Q447639|lt=Ashikaga Yoshihisa|1=Ashikaga Yoshihisa}} in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2023-03-30 |publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208205908/https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |archive-date=2022-12-08}}</ref>

== Grounds ==
Main buildings include:
* Main Hall - Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station|1=Kusatsu Station (Shiga)}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)|1=Arashi}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno|1=Satoshi Ohno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>

{{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>

'''Ōno Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Rittō|en|Rittō|WD=Q3937811|lt=Rittō|1=Rittō}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |title=Ōno Shrine Festival |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Nihon Kankō Shinkō Kyōkai |archive-date=2018-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181119173520/http://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shiga/detail/25321ba2210118922 |url-status=unfit |url-status-date=2023-03-30}}</ref> Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|鄉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Enshrined Deity ==
* Principal deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2018-11-18 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201020223615/http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=383 |archive-date=2020-10-20}}</ref>

== History ==
The exact date of establishment is unknown, but classical sources indicate that the shrine existed before {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō|1=Gangyō}} 6 (882).<ref name="History">[https://ohnojinja.shiga.jp/origin Official Website of Ōno Shrine]</ref> Originally, it was founded as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Konkō-ji (Rittō)|de|Konkō-ji|ja|金勝寺 (栗東市)|zh|金胜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konkō-ji (Rittō)|1=Konsho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646560}} temple during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It was later associated with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} in 897 and became a prominent site for {{ill|Tenjin worship|de|Tenjin-Verehrung|ja|天神信仰|zh|天神信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin worship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tenjin faith|WD=Q66890725}}.<ref name="History"/>

Throughout its history, the shrine was patronized by powerful clans such as the Sasaki (Rokkaku) family during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, and by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|en|Ashikaga Yoshihisa|WD=Q447639|lt=Ashikaga Yoshihisa|1=Ashikaga Yoshihisa}} in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |title=Ōno Shrine |accessdate=2023-03-30 |publisher=Biwako Visitors Bureau |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208205908/https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/171/ |archive-date=2022-12-08}}</ref>

== Grounds ==
Main buildings include:
* Main Hall - Sangensha {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
* Heiden (offering hall)
* Worship Hall (prayer hall)
* Kannon Hall - Houses a wooden statue of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (city-designated cultural property)
* Important Cultural Property: Rōmon (Gate)

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Rōmon gate (designated March 27, 1901)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1432 |title=Rōmon of Ōno Shrine |access-date=2023-03-30 |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

=== City-designated Properties ===
* Three-Shrine Hall
* Wooden Eleven-faced Kannon Statue - Heian period
* Waniguchi gong - Muromachi period

== Access ==
From JR {{ill|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|en|Kusatsu Station (Shiga)|WD=Q775813|lt=Kusatsu Station|1=Kusatsu Station (Shiga)}} by Teisan Konan Kotsu bus:
* 500m walk from "Ōno Jinja Sandō Iriguchi" stop

== Other Information ==
The shrine has attracted fans of {{ill|Arashi|en|Arashi|WD=Q626440|lt=Arashi (group)|1=Arashi}}'s leader, {{ill|Satoshi Ohno|en|Satoshi Ohno|WD=Q362271|lt=Satoshi Ōno|1=Satoshi Ohno}}, due to sharing his name, leading to themed merchandise being produced.<ref>{{Cite news|title=嵐ファンの聖地 大野神社で「馬勝った守」 |newspaper=Sankei Shinbun |date=2013-12-11}}</ref>
